name: Climb to the Faro de la Mola - round trip crossing Formentera
name_original: Subida al Faro de la Mola — ida y vuelta cruzando Formentera
region_intro: Road cycling around Formentera is characterized by its largely flat topography, making it accessible for various fitness levels. The island features a network of well-signposted routes, often utilizing old rural roads, that traverse diverse landscapes. Cyclists can explore coastal beauty, rural areas with vineyards and traditional architecture, and significant natural features like salt pans and lakes. The ascent to La Mola offers a more challenging climb with panoramic views. Best road cycling routes around Formentera The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the Faro de la Mola - round trip crossing Formentera, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the iconic La Mola Lighthouse, offering dramatic cliff-top views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Sant Francesc de Formentera to Faro de Barbaria — return via the Ses Salines Natural Park, an easy 21.0 miles (33.7 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes through the protected Ses Salines Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the View of Formentera – Salt Lagoon loop from Molí d’en Botigues, a 21.9 miles (35.2 km) trail leading through salt pans and along the transparent waters of Estany des Falmencs, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Road cycling around Formentera is defined by coastal paths, rural landscapes with vineyards, and iconic lighthouses. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Formentera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Formentera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Formentera?
A: Formentera is largely characterized by its flat topography, making it accessible for cyclists of all levels. While most routes offer gentle riding, the ascent to La Mola provides a more challenging climb with rewarding panoramic views. You'll find routes utilizing old rural roads, traversing diverse landscapes from coastal paths to rural areas with vineyards.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Formentera?
A: There are nearly 10 dedicated road cycling routes around Formentera listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences. These include easy rides and moderate challenges, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes in Formentera?
A: Yes, while much of Formentera is flat, the climb to La Mola offers a more challenging experience. A popular route for this is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000273182/climb-to-the-faro-de-la-mola-round-trip-crossing-formentera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Faro de la Mola - round trip crossing Formentera&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as moderate and features significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can visit while cycling?
A: Formentera offers numerous scenic spots. You can cycle to iconic lighthouses like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5736241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbaria Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dramatic cliff-top views, or the La Mola Lighthouse. Routes also pass through the Ses Salines Natural Park, offering views of pine trees and dunes, and along the transparent waters of Estany des Falmencs, perfect for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Formentera?
A: Yes, Formentera has several excellent circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14680546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Formentera – Salt Lagoon loop from Molí d’en Botigues&lt;/a&gt; takes you through salt pans and along the lake, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap de Barbaria Lighthouse loop from Sant Ferran de ses Roques&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning coastal views.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about routes in Formentera?
A: The road cycling routes in Formentera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the island's largely flat and accessible terrain, the well-signposted Green Routes, and the diverse landscapes that include coastal beauty, rural charm, and iconic lighthouses.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Absolutely. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000271212/from-sant-francesc-de-formentera-to-faro-de-barbaria-return-via-the-ses-salines-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sant Francesc de Formentera to Faro de Barbaria — return via the Ses Salines Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you directly through the protected Ses Salines Natural Park, offering beautiful coastal views and a chance to observe local wildlife.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or less experienced cyclists?
A: Formentera's largely flat topography makes it ideal for families and cyclists of varying fitness levels. Many of the island's 32 'Green Routes' are specifically suitable for cycling and offer gentle rides away from motorized traffic. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000271212/from-sant-francesc-de-formentera-to-faro-de-barbaria-return-via-the-ses-salines-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sant Francesc de Formentera to Faro de Barbaria&lt;/a&gt; are rated as easy.

Q: What kind of natural features can I explore on a road bike in Formentera?
A: You can explore a variety of natural features, including breathtaking coves and beaches like Ses Illetes, ancient salt pans, and lakes such as Estany des Falmencs, which is a non-bathing area perfect for observing migratory birds like flamingos. The island's diverse landscapes also include pine forests and dunes.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to beaches or coves?
A: Yes, several routes offer access to Formentera's stunning coastline. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Formentera – Cala Saona Beach loop from Molí d’en Botigues&lt;/a&gt; will take you towards the beautiful Cala Saona Beach, known for its crystal-clear waters.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Formentera?
A: Formentera is an excellent destination for road cycling for much of the year. The mild Mediterranean climate means pleasant temperatures outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable conditions for cycling, with fewer crowds and ideal weather for exploring the island.

Q: Can I see any historical or cultural sites while cycling?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can discover historical elements such as the dolmen of Can Na Costa, an impressive archaeological find, and traditional fishermen's huts. Routes often traverse areas with traditional architecture, providing a glimpse into the island's rural character.
tips: The only asphalted climb that goes from the center of Es Caló to Pilar de la Mola along more than three and a half kilometers and rises just to see the panorama of the west of the island, with the two most famous beaches on both sides: Platja des Mitgjorn and Tramuntana.
The lighthouse houses a museum space dedicated to maritime heritage, presenting exhibitions on the history of navigation and the role of lighthouses.

waypoints.name: Ascent to Pilar de la Mola
